Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Thomas B. Mich's Motion to Compel Arbitration is GRANTED.

(See word minutesa for complete detail.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

PLAINTIFF THOMAS MICH AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2025 Off Calendar

Cause Of Action: Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Thomas B. Mich's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Party's motion was neither timely filed nor timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b); Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail to place outside State adds ten court days to "any period of notice"].) see also Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Nor did Moving Party timely file proper proof of service of the moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) Thus, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances :
Thomas Mich sel-represented plaintiff.
Arthur C. Lipton, esq., of LIPTON LAW OFFICE Tel:(415) 658-7873 for defendant. (via Zoom Conference)

Hearing is held. The court didnt receive a contest email for this matter. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Thomas Mich's motion for a preliminary injunction is denied.

Mr. Mich has known about the likely sale of Phonobar for at least a year. There is no evidence that prior to filing this lawsuit Mr. Mich made any effort to purchase Phonobar per his claimed right of first refusal. Because of his inaction in pursuing his claimed right of first refusal, Mr. Mich has not shown that he likely to prevail on his claim that his contractual rights have been violated by the sale of Phonobar. At this time, with the ABC license transferred to the purchaser and escrow likely to close in the near future, it would cause significant and likely irreparable harm to the purchaser, Concept Kismet, and Mr. Lukezic if the sale was restrained. Mr. Mich has not shown that, if he ultimately succeeds on any of his claims, money damages is inadequate to remediate any harm that he has suffered.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)
